expectations.text: Identifies that the documented access policies (public/authenticated/owner/cel) don't match the real enum (public-read/authenticated/owner-only)
expectations.evidence: Finding #1 (high): "Access-policy table lists public / authenticated / owner / cel. Actual enum ... is exactly public-read / authenticated / owner-only. public, owner, and cel are not valid values."

expectations.text: Identifies that the documented TTL tiers (1-hour/1-day/30-days/persistent) don't match the real tiers (1d/3d/14d/28d/180d/365d/permanent)
expectations.evidence: Finding #4 (high): "TTL-tier table values 1-hour / 1-day / 30-days / persistent are all fictional. Actual BlobBucketTtlTier ... is 1d / 3d / 14d / 28d / 180d / 365d / permanent."

expectations.text: Identifies that the [blobBucket] TOML table name and/or the inline [blobBucket.rules] CEL block don't match the real sync format ([bucket]; CEL rules not a released bucket surface)
expectations.evidence: Finding #8 (table name [blobBucket]→[bucket], displayName→name) plus finding #5: "The [blobBucket.rules] table with read/write/delete keys does not exist — sync only accepts a ruleSetId string."

expectations.text: Identifies the metadata-vs-tags mismatch (uploads accept tags: string[]; there is no arbitrary metadata field for CEL rules to reference)
expectations.evidence: Finding #7: "The real upload surface takes tags: string[] (BlobUploadParams.tags) ... the API 'has no arbitrary metadata field.'"

expectations.text: Flags the shipped 'Content review needed' warning box as something that must not be published
expectations.evidence: Finding #7 explicitly flags "a ::: warning Content review needed admitting the API ... STYLE.md 'No inconsistency asides' ... Remove the parenthetical (105) and the warning callout (115-117)."

expectations.text: Does not invent additional false facts (no claims contradicting the source it cites)
expectations.evidence: "What is correct" section confirms the example-corpus includes exist and are parity-clean ("all referenced files exist"), the 100MB limit, CLI flags, and workflow steps — all consistent with ground truth. No false facts found; the false claim seen in the baseline (example files missing) is explicitly contradicted here.

claims.claim: Signed-URL expiry is capped at 24 hours, not 7 days
claims.evidence: Finding #6 cites Math.min(requested, 60*60*24)=24h; matches ground truth (signed-URL cap is 24h).

claims.claim: The referenced examples/blobs/bucket-* include files exist and are parity-clean
claims.evidence: "all referenced files exist and the JS/Swift blocks are corpus-pair" — matches ground truth that the example files do exist at the repo examples/ directory.

claims.claim: owner-only excludes the uploader (only admin/owner can read)
claims.evidence: Finding #2 cites checkReadAccess; surfaced as a bug-to-file. Beyond the assertions but consistent with the cited source.

eval_feedback.overall: No suggestions, evals look solid. The 'does not invent false facts' assertion is well-chosen — it discriminated between the two runs (the baseline failed it by claiming the include files don't exist; this run passed by correctly verifying they do).
